chapter_title.en: Chapter on Judgment Regarding the Enclosure Between Two Houses

text: 3413 - وَ رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ قَضَى فِي رَجُلَيْنِ اِخْتَصَمَا إِلَيْهِ فِي خُصٍّ فَقَالَ "إِنَّ اَلْخُصَّ لِلَّذِي إِلَيْهِ اَلْقُمُطُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3413 - Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his father, from his grandfather, from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as):
He ruled in a case where two men disputed over a reed fence (khuss).
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "The fence belongs to the one to whom the boundary rope (al-qimat) is attached."
